response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web engineer, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a thesis project, far short of the 5–10 years required. His entire career arc is TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web infrastructure — with no evidence of Windows platform development, SQL Server or Oracle depth, or any exposure to capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking systems. The RIDE Capital stint is fintech-adjacent but not capital markets. No desirable skills such as Summit, STL, Python, Kafka, or messaging frameworks appear.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps experience across multiple roles, including GitLab, Docker, and Artifactory
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record through public talks, workshops, and teaching engagements
Breadth of engineering experience across diverse domains and tech stacks, indicating fast adaptability
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months of professional use — roughly 90% short of the 5–10 year mandatory requirement
Zero domain knowledge in fixed income, fixed income derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking
No indication of Windows platform development; career history is predominantly Linux and web-oriented with no SQL Server or Oracle usage

key_factors.factor: No capital markets or fixed income domain knowledge
